title: Creamy Crusty Russian Squash Flatbread
description: A fresh and flaky Russian dish featuring roasted squash on a double-rise bread, perfect for a brunch mood and FODMAP friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the flour, yeast, salt, and sugar.
Add the melted butter, yogurt, and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Let the dough rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Punch down the dough and let it rise for another 30 minutes.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch and place on a baking sheet.
Toss the squash with a little bit of oil and season with salt and garlic free seasoning, then spread over the dough.
Sprinkle the chopped dill over the squash.
Bake the flatbread in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown.
